pageDescription: Calculate the ballistic coefficient of a projectile for precision shooting and trajectory analysis

definition.title: Ballistic Coefficient Definition
definition.description: A measure of a bullet's ability to overcome air resistance in flight compared to a standard projectile

formula.title: Calculation Formula
formula.description: BC = Sectional Density ÷ Form Factor, where SD = Weight ÷ Diameter²

standards.title: Standard Models
standards.description: G1 (flat-based) and G7 (boat-tail) are the most common reference models

criticalWarningsList.notDiagnostic: This calculator provides estimates only and is not a substitute for empirical testing
criticalWarningsList.environmental: Actual performance varies with environmental conditions and barrel characteristics

importantNotesList: Higher BC values indicate better aerodynamic efficiency
G7 standard is more accurate for modern boat-tail bullets
Manufacturing tolerances can affect actual BC values

messages.enterInformation: Enter your bullet specifications to calculate ballistic coefficient
messages.resultMessage: Your ballistic coefficient calculation is shown below

disclaimer.title: Important Disclaimer
disclaimer.content: This calculator provides ballistic coefficient estimates based on mathematical formulas. Actual performance may vary based on environmental conditions, barrel characteristics, and manufacturing tolerances.

seekHelp.title: Ballistics Resources
seekHelp.content: For professional ballistics analysis and precision shooting applications, consult ballistics experts or use specialized software like Applied Ballistics or JBM Ballistics.

errors.bulletWeight: Please enter a valid bullet weight
errors.bulletDiameter: Please enter a valid bullet diameter
errors.bulletLength: Please enter a valid bullet length

tooltips.bulletType: Type of bullet affects form factor

enter_projectile_parameters_to_calculate: Enter projectile parameters to calculate ballistic coefficient
area_will_be_calculated_as_d2_18: Area will be calculated as π × (d/2)²
b_ballistic_coefficient_kgm_m_mass_kg_cd:
B = Ballistic Coefficient (kg/m²), m = mass (kg), Cd = drag coefficient,
                        A = area (m²)
ballistic_coefficient_bc_is_a_measure_of:
Ballistic coefficient (BC) is a measure of a projectile's ability to overcome air resistance in
                      flight. Higher BC values indicate better aerodynamic efficiency and less velocity loss over
                      distance.
sd_m_a_mass_per_unit_area_56: SD = m / A (mass per unit area)
better_longrange_performance_less_wind_d: Better long-range performance, less wind drift
